title: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) - Action report (23/03/2026) - Communication from North Macedonia concerning the case of K.M. v. North Macedonia (Application No. 59144/16) [anglais uniquement)
text: SECRETARIAT / SECRÉTARIAT SECRETARIAT OF THE COMMITTEE OF MINISTERS SECRÉTARIAT DU COMITÉ DES MINISTRES Contact: Ireneusz Kondak Tel: 03.90.21.59.86 Date: 23/03/2026 DH-DD(2026)381 Documents distributed at the request of a Representative shall be under the sole responsibility of the said Representative, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. Meeting: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) Item reference: Action Report (23/03/2026) Communication from North Macedonia concerning the case of K.M. v. North Macedonia (Application No. 59144/16) * * * * * * * * * * * Les documents distribués à la demande d’un/e Représentant/e le sont sous la seule responsabilité dudit/de ladite Représentant/e, sans préjuger de la position juridique ou politique du Comité des Ministres. Réunion : 1563e réunion (juin 2026) (DH) Référence du point : Bilan d’action (23/03/2026) Communication de la Macédoine du Nord concernant l’affaire K.M. c. Macédoine du Nord (requête n° 59144/16) (anglais uniquement) Skopje, 23 March 2026 ACTION REPORT K.M. v. North Macedonia Application no. 59144/16 Judgment of 4 March 2025, final on 4 June 2025 I CASE DESCRIPTION 1. The case concerns the alleged sexual abuse of the applicant, a fourteen-year-old girl at the material time in 2013, and her inability to obtain effective protection against the alleged violation of her personal integrity (violation of Article 8). 2. The Court held that the domestic criminal-law framework in force at the material time did not offer any protection against non-consensual sexual acts performed, without using force or threats, on a person who had turned 14, as was the case with the applicant (§ 72). Similarly, the domestic civil-law provisions, as applied in practice by the domestic courts, did not afford the applicant the necessary protection against sexual abuse (§ 77). II INDIVIDUAL MEASURES 3. The authorities have taken measures to bring the violation to an end. They are set out below. A. Reopening of the impugned proceedings 4. At the outset, the authorities would like to indicate that the domestic legislation provided for a concrete and effective avenue to obtain re-examination of the applicant’s case. In particular, provision of Section 400 of the Civil Proceedings Act, provides a possibility to apply for reopening of the proceedings following a judgment of the Court finding a violation. The reopening could be sought within thirty days after the Court’s judgment is final. To the best knowledge of the Government the applicant has not filed a request for reopening of the impugned proceedings. 5. At the same time, in view of the nullum crimen sine lege principle (§ 71), the applicant cannot rely on the new legislation (see paragraphs 6-9 below) regarding the alleged incident which took place in 2013. Action report | K.M. 2 B. The applicant’s redress 6. The applicant claimed EUR 30.000 in respect of non-pecuniary damage (§ 80). The Court consi dered that the applicant must have suffered non-pecuniary damage as a result of the State`s failure to protect her physical and psychological integrity and awarded the applicant EUR 4, 500 in respect of non-pecuniary damage (§ 82). 7. The applicant has therefore been properly redressed in respect of the damage sustained. III GENERAL MEASURES 8. Necessary measures have been taken to prevent similar violations. They are set out below. A. Legislative changes in the domestic criminal law framework 6. It is recalled that the Court held that the domestic criminal-law framework in force at the material time did not offer any protection against non-consensual sexual acts performed, without using force or threats, on a person who had turned 14, as was the case with the applicant (§ 72). Similarly, the domestic civil-law provisions, as applied in practice by the domestic courts, did not afford the applicant the necessary protection against sexual abuse (§ 77). 7. It is also recalled that the Court reiterated that the effective protection against sexual abuse requires measures of a criminal-law nature (§ 52) and that it found that the seriousness of the acts in question required a criminal law response (§ 68). 9. The Court also took note of the subsequent entry into force of the Istanbul Convention in respect of North Macedonia in 2018 and the amendments made to the Criminal Code in February 2023, which criminalise acts such as those in the present case (§ 71). As a result of these legislative changes, a non-consensual sexual act not constituting penetration is now punishable by a term of between one and three years’ imprisonment if committed against a person above the age of 15 (Article 186 § 7) and at least three years’ imprisonment if committed against a person below the age of 15 (Article 188 § 5). The amendments also introduced the offence of sexual harassment (полово вознемирување – Article 190-a). 10. On 7 September 2023 the Council of Europe Group of Experts on Action against Violence against Women and Domestic Violence (GREVIO) published its (Baseline) Evaluation Report in respect of North Macedonia in which it positively evaluated the adopted legislation, noting inter alia that “it welcomes the amendments to the elements of the offences set out in Article 186, which are fully in line with the requirement under Article 36 of the Istanbul Convention to criminalise all non-consensual sexual acts.”1 1 GREVIO/Inf(2023)5, § 259. Action report | K.M. 3 B. Civil law framework 11. It is recalled that, under the established case-law of the European Court. the effective protection against serious violations of Articles 3 and 8 such as sexual abuse primarily requires measures of a criminal-law nature. As noted above (paragraph § 9), such Convention-compliant legislative framework has now been adopted. At the same time, it is recalled that in the very specific circumstances of this case (§ 53) the Court also considered the adequacy of civil-law remedies and it held that the domestic civil-law provisions, as applied in practice by the domestic courts, did not afford the applicant the necessary protection against sexual abuse (§ 77). 12. The authorities consider that this judgment presents an isolated occurrence of erroneous application of existing civil legal framework, which in fact provides for the possibility of civil compensation for protection of personal rights under sections 9-a and 189 of the Obligations Act, which the domestic courts in this particular case erroneously had not taken into account Considering the specific circumstances of the present case and the fact that there are no other cases raising allegations of similar violations currently pending before the European Court, the authorities consider that further general measures presented below will be sufficient to prevent similar violation. C. Publication and dissemination measures 6. The authorities provided publication of the Court’s judgment in the case at hand in order to make sure that the domestic authorities, including domestic judges and prosecutors, are aware of and comply with the findings of the Court’s case at hand. The Court’s judgment in the present case has, in particular, been published in Macedonian and posted on the website of the Bureau for Representation of the Republic of North Macedonia before the European Court of Human Rights (www.biroescp.gov.mk), making it available to the public. 7. With a view to facilitating dissemination of the judgment and making it accessible to the legal professionals in North Macedonia, the Office of the Government Agent prepared an analysis of the Court’s judgment highlighting its most important findings. The Government Agent ensured that the above-mentioned analysis and the Court’s judgment were transmitted to all relevant judicial authorities and other competent bodies (including, but not limited to the Constitutional Court, the Supreme Court; the four Courts of Appeal, the Judicial Council; the Public Prosecutor`s Office and to the four Higher Prosecutor`s Office, the Ombudsman’s Office; the Academy for Training of Judges and Public Prosecutors; the Macedonian Judges’ Association; the Bar Association, the Association of Public Prosecutors and the Ministry of Justice). 8. In view of the above, the authorities consider that the above-mentioned measures will be capable of preventing similar violations. Action report | K.M. 4 IV JUST SATISFACTION 9. On 21.08.2025 the authorities ensured that the just satisfaction awarded by the Court has been paid to the applicant. Thus, the payment has been made within the deadline set out by the European Court. V CONCLUSIONS 10. The authorities deem that the general measures taken and envisaged will be capable of preventing similar violations. 11. The Government thus deems that all measures required by Article 46 § 1 of the Convention have been implemented. Action report | K.M. 5
case_name: CASE OF K.M. v. NORTH MACEDONIA
